growthcleanr 2.99.0
  • Articles
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Clean growth measurements

Source: R/child_clean.R
cleangrowth.Rd

Two calling styles are supported:

cleangrowth(
  data = NULL,
  subjid = NULL,
  param = NULL,
  agedays = NULL,
  sex = NULL,
  measurement = NULL,
  piv.z.wt.high = 22,
  piv.z.ht.low.young = -25,
  piv.z.ht.low.old = -15,
  piv.z.ht.high = 8,
  piv.z.hc.low = -15,
  piv.z.hc.high = 15,
  error.load.mincount = 2,
  error.load.threshold = 0.5,
  sd.recenter = NA,
  recenter_source = "reference",
  cf_rescue = "standard",
  cf_detail = FALSE,
  corr_detail = FALSE,
  recenter_detail = FALSE,
  ref.data.path = "",
  log.path = NA,
  parallel = FALSE,
  num.batches = NA,
  quietly = TRUE,
  verbose = FALSE,
  adult_cutpoint = 20,
  adult_permissiveness = "looser",
  adult_scale_max_lbs = Inf,
  ewma_window = 15,
  id = NULL,
  ref_tables = NULL,
  cached_results = NULL,
  changed_subjids = NULL,
  tri_exclude = FALSE,
  debug = FALSE,
  full_detail = FALSE,
  unit_error_range = TRUE,
  display_gc_id = FALSE,
  exclude_detail = FALSE,
  batch_size = 5000,
  length.adjust = FALSE,
  .checkpoint_driven = FALSE
)

Arguments

data

Optional data.frame or data.table containing the input measurements. When supplied, subjid, param, agedays, sex, measurement, and id are interpreted as column-name strings (defaulting to the canonical names). When NULL (the default), the function uses the legacy vector-style API.

subjid

Subject identifier. In data-frame style, a column name (default "subjid"). In vector style, a vector of unique subject identifiers - one entry per measurement row.

param

Measurement parameter. In data-frame style, a column name (default "param"). In vector style, a vector with values "WEIGHTKG", "WEIGHTLBS", "HEIGHTCM", "HEIGHTIN", "LENGTHCM", or "HEADCM". "HEIGHTCM"/"HEIGHTIN" vs. "LENGTHCM" only affects z-score calculations between ages 24 to 35 months (730 to 1095 days). All linear measurements below 731 days of life (age 0-23 months) are interpreted as supine length, and all linear measurements above 1095 days (age 36+ months) are interpreted as standing height. Note: at the moment, all LENGTHCM will be converted to HEIGHTCM. See length.adjust for an optional correction for recumbent-labeled measurements taken after age 2 years. Imperial "HEIGHTIN" and "WEIGHTLBS" measurements are converted to metric during algorithm calculations.

agedays

Age in days at each measurement. In data-frame style, a column name (default "agedays"). In vector style, a numeric vector.

sex

Subject sex. In data-frame style, a column name (default "sex"). In vector style, a vector; values may be "M", "m", or 0 for males, and "F", "f", or 1 for females.

measurement

Recorded measurement. In data-frame style, a column name (default "measurement"). In vector style, a numeric vector. Weight must be in kilograms (kg), and linear measurements in centimeters (cm), unless param is "WEIGHTLBS" or "HEIGHTIN".

piv.z.wt.high

Upper unrecentered CSD z-score cutoff for WEIGHTKG (all ages). Weights with sd.orig_uncorr > piv.z.wt.high are flagged as standardized PIV. Defaults to 22.

piv.z.ht.low.young

Lower unrecentered CSD z-score cutoff for HEIGHTCM under one year of age. Heights with sd.orig_uncorr < piv.z.ht.low.young are flagged as standardized PIV. Defaults to -25.

piv.z.ht.low.old

Lower unrecentered CSD z-score cutoff for HEIGHTCM at or above one year. Heights with sd.orig_uncorr < piv.z.ht.low.old are flagged as standardized PIV. Defaults to -15.

piv.z.ht.high

Upper unrecentered CSD z-score cutoff for HEIGHTCM (all ages). Heights with sd.orig_uncorr > piv.z.ht.high are flagged as standardized PIV. Defaults to 8 (tighter than the weight upper limit, based on analysis of CHOP data showing the +/-15/+/-25 range was too loose for heights).

piv.z.hc.low

Lower unrecentered CSD z-score cutoff for HEADCM (all ages). Head-circumference values with sd.orig_uncorr < piv.z.hc.low are flagged as standardized PIV. Defaults to -15.

piv.z.hc.high

Upper unrecentered CSD z-score cutoff for HEADCM (all ages). Head-circumference values with sd.orig_uncorr > piv.z.hc.high are flagged as standardized PIV. Defaults to 15.

error.load.mincount

Minimum number of real errors (exclusions other than Exclude-C-Identical, Exclude-C-Extraneous, Exclude-C-CF, Exclude-Missing, and Exclude-Not-Cleaned) required per (subject, param) group before Child Step 21 Error Load is evaluated for that group. Defaults to 2.

error.load.threshold

Error ratio threshold for Child Step 21. The ratio is n_errors / (n_errors + n_includes) computed per (subject, param) group, where n_errors and n_includes exclude SDE / CF / Missing / Not-Cleaned rows from both numerator and denominator. If the ratio strictly exceeds this threshold (and at least error.load.mincount real errors are present), all remaining Include rows for that (subject, param) group are excluded with Exclude-C-Unevaluable-Trajectory. Defaults to 0.5.

sd.recenter

Optional. A data.table with columns param, sex, agedays, and rcmedian.sd to use for recentering instead of the built-in reference file (rcfile-resmoothed.csv.gz). A legacy table whose median column is named sd.median is still accepted. Defaults to NA, which uses the built-in file.

recenter_source

How to obtain the recentering medians when sd.recenter is not a supplied table. One of "reference" (default; the built-in large-site reference file) or "none" (no recentering: the recentered scores equal the reference scores, tbc.sd = sd.orig). A sd.recenter data.table always takes precedence over this argument.

cf_rescue

CF rescue mode for the child algorithm. One of:

"standard"

(Default) Decide each identical-value string on its last carried value (highest seq_win); the whole string is rescued or excluded together. When the last CF has a next included value, rescue if cf_deltaz + cf_nextz <= 1 and cf_deltaz <= 1.5; when it has no next value, rescue by age (cf_deltaz <= 0.5 if agedays < 13*365.25, else cf_deltaz <= 1). cf_deltaz = |sd.orig_uncorr - originator_z|. These interim rules replaced the former lookup-table thresholds on 2026-07-01 and will be refined after the updates-beta release.

"none"

No rescue. All identical-to-prior values are excluded as CF.

"all"

All identical-to-prior values are rescued (no CF exclusions).

cf_detail

Logical. If TRUE, include cf_status, cf_deltaz, cf_nextz, imperial, cf_dage_orig, and cf_dage_next columns in the output. Every row gets a cf_status: "Orig" (originator of a carried-forward string), "CF-R" (carried forward, rescued), "CF-NR" (carried forward, not rescued), "CF-E" (CF on a day that also has an Include – a same-day extraneous CF, never rescue-eligible), "Not-CF" (no CF relationship), or "Adult" (adult row; CF detection not applicable). cf_deltaz is the absolute z-score difference (sd.orig_uncorr) between the CF and its originator – the value used for rescue evaluation (NA except for carried-forward values; CF-E rows do not participate in string detection and so have NA). cf_nextz is the signed change in sd.orig_uncorr from a CF row (CF-R or CF-NR) to the next non-CF value that is Included at the time of Step 6 (temp same-day extraneous values do not count). NA for non-CF rows, for CF-E rows, and when no eligible next value exists. imperial is a row-level logical flag: TRUE when the value is a whole number of pounds (WEIGHTKG) or a whole/half inch (HEIGHTCM/HEADCM), within 0.01 of the imperial unit. cf_dage_orig is the day gap from a CF row's originator to the CF row (agedays_CF - agedays_orig; always positive); populated for CF-R/CF-NR rows, NA otherwise. cf_dage_next is the day gap from a CF row (CF-R/CF-NR) to the next eligible non-CF Included value (agedays_next - agedays_CF; always positive); NA for non-CF rows, CF-E rows, and at the end of a subject/param series. These three columns are design aids for CF-logic development. Defaults to FALSE.

corr_detail

Logical. If TRUE, include corr_val and corr_pt columns in the output. corr_val labels each value "Corrected" (its gestational-age-corrected z-score differs from the uncorrected one), "Uncorrected", or "Adult" (adult row). corr_pt labels each subject "Corrected" (the subject has at least one corrected value), "Uncorrected", or "Adult". Defaults to FALSE.

recenter_detail

Logical. If TRUE, include the recentered CSD z-score columns in the output: tbc.sd (the recentered z used throughout the child algorithm), ctbc.sd (its gestational-age-corrected counterpart), and rcmedian.sd (the per-row recentering median that was subtracted). This is a stable, supported output contract for downstream tools (e.g. quality-measure code) that read the recentered z-score - unlike debug, which is diagnostic and free to change. The three columns are a subset of what debug surfaces, exposed here under their own named switch. Child rows only; adult rows receive NA. Defaults to FALSE. Also turned on by full_detail = TRUE.

ref.data.path

Path to reference data. If not supplied, the year 2000 Centers for Disease Control (CDC) reference data will be used.

log.path

Path to log file output when running in parallel (non-quiet mode). Default is NA. A new directory will be created if necessary. Set to NA to disable log files.

parallel

Determines if function runs in parallel. Defaults to FALSE.

num.batches

Specify the number of batches to run in parallel. Only applies if parallel is set to TRUE. Defaults to the number of workers returned by the getDoParWorkers function in the foreach package.

quietly

Determines if function messages are to be displayed and if log files (parallel only) are to be generated. Defaults to TRUE

verbose

When TRUE, prints granular per-step diagnostics (pre-filter counts, EWMA/SDE iteration counts, etc.) on top of the standard progress milestones. Implies quietly = FALSE. Intended for development and debugging. Defaults to FALSE.

adult_cutpoint

Number between 18 and 20, describing ages when the pediatric algorithm should not be applied (< adult_cutpoint), and the adult algorithm should apply (>= adult_cutpoint). Numbers outside this range will be changed to the closest number within the range. Defaults to 20.

adult_permissiveness

Permissiveness level for the adult algorithm: "loosest", "looser" (default), "tighter", or "tightest". Sets defaults for all adult PIV limits, EWMA caps, height bands, etc. See adult_clean.R for sub-parameter details.

adult_scale_max_lbs

Physical scale upper limit in pounds for adult weight data. Weights at or above this value are excluded. Defaults to Inf.

ewma_window

Number of measurements on each side included in the exponentially weighted moving average (EWMA) weighting window. Defaults to 15.

id

Row identifier. Fully optional. In data-frame style, a column name (default "id"); in vector style, a vector of row ids. If id is absent / NULL / contains NA / is not unique, a warning is issued and sequential integer ids (1:N in input row order) are auto-generated for internal use. The user's original id (whatever was supplied - even if NA or duplicated) is preserved in the output id column so callers can map results back to their data. To see the id that growthcleanr actually used internally, set display_gc_id = TRUE (or full_detail = TRUE); the output then includes a gc_id column equal to the user's id when no regen was needed, or the auto-generated 1:N when a regen occurred. For stable row identifiers, supply a unique per-row id; a per-subject or per-encounter identifier is not a valid id for this purpose.

ref_tables

Optional. Pre-loaded reference closures from gc_preload_refs. When provided, skips all read_anthro() file reads (~0.93 sec per call on 200 subjects; ~13 hours saved over 50K calls). Recommended for repeated calls (e.g., simulation loops). Defaults to NULL (reads files each call).

cached_results

Deprecated and disabled (2026-06-22). Partial (incremental) runs are turned off pending a redesign after the next release. Supplying cached_results now emits a warning and triggers a full clean of all subjects (results are correct, just not incremental) - the previous cache-merge path returned unchanged subjects with their data columns blanked to NA and was unsafe. The argument is retained for signature compatibility; it will be reintroduced with a corrected design. Defaults to NULL (process all subjects).

changed_subjids

Deprecated and disabled (2026-06-22). See cached_results. Has no effect; a full clean is run regardless. Defaults to NULL.

tri_exclude

Logical. If TRUE, include a tri_exclude column in the output that classifies each row as "Include", "Same-Day" (same-day extraneous values), or "Exclude" (all other exclusions). Defaults to FALSE.

debug

Logical. If TRUE, retain intermediate diagnostic columns in the output instead of trimming to the public columns. Intended for threshold refinement and development, not routine use. Currently surfaces the raw and recentered CSD z-scores captured before cleaning (sd.orig, sd.corr, tbc.sd, ctbc.sd) and the recentered plus/minus perturbation z-scores (tbc.p_plus, tbc.p_minus, ctbc.p_plus, ctbc.p_minus); additional per-step diagnostic columns are added here as the child trajectory steps gain them. Adult rows receive NA for these child-only columns. Defaults to FALSE.

full_detail

Logical. Convenience switch: if TRUE, turns on all optional output groups at once (equivalent to cf_detail = TRUE, corr_detail = TRUE, debug = TRUE, tri_exclude = TRUE, display_gc_id = TRUE, recenter_detail = TRUE, and exclude_detail = TRUE). Defaults to FALSE. Does not affect unit_error_range, which is on by default independently.

unit_error_range

Logical. If TRUE (default), include a unit_error_range column: a direction-agnostic flag that is TRUE when a value's magnitude is consistent with a metric/imperial unit-conversion error and the value is not itself within the plausible z = -3 to +3 band. Purely informational – it never affects the exclude decision and is not an exclusion code. Computed only for cleaned rows (Exclude-Missing / Exclude-Not-Cleaned rows are FALSE). Set FALSE to omit the column.

display_gc_id

Logical. If TRUE, add a gc_id column to the output containing the id that growthcleanr actually used internally: the user's id when it was valid (no regen needed), or the auto-generated 1:N (in input row order) when the user's id was absent / NA / non-unique and growthcleanr had to regenerate. Lets callers recover a reliable per-row key when their input id had problems (the output id column always preserves whatever the user supplied, even if invalid). Defaults to FALSE. Set via full_detail = TRUE alongside the other detail switches.

exclude_detail

Logical. If TRUE, add an exclude_detailed column reporting each value's exclusion at the Detailed level: step-level codes that keep the child (-C-) / adult (-A-) algorithm prefix but fold away repeated-value and round-iteration variants. The default exclude column always reports the consolidated Summary level. Both share the same shared/preprocessing codes. Defaults to FALSE.

batch_size

Number of subjects per processing batch. Subjects are never split across batches. Defaults to 5000.

length.adjust

Logical. If TRUE, subtracts 0.7 cm from all LENGTHCM measurements with agedays > 2 * 365.25 (age > 2 years) before z-score calculation. Recumbent length is approximately 0.7 cm longer than standing height for the same child; if a facility records older children's heights as LENGTHCM (recumbent), this correction converts them to the standing equivalent assumed by the WHO/CDC reference standards at those ages. Has no effect for agedays <= 2 * 365.25 (where recumbent is the expected measurement method). Defaults to FALSE.

.checkpoint_driven

Internal. Set to TRUE by cleangrowth_checkpoint so that the per-chunk header, banner, and elapsed-time framing are printed by the checkpoint wrapper instead of here (this call then prints only its milestone lines). Not for direct use.

Value

A data.table with eight default columns: id (user-provided row identifier), subjid, param, agedays, sex, measurement, exclude (exclusion code or "Include"), and bin_exclude ("Include" or "Exclude"). Output fidelity: the five echoed input columns (subjid, param, agedays, sex, measurement) carry the user's verbatim submitted values - gc never silently alters or drops an input value. Reinterpretations needed to run the algorithm are internal-only: imperial param/measurement are returned as submitted (not converted to metric), LENGTHCM is returned as LENGTHCM (not relabeled), a submitted sex code is returned as submitted (gc's coerced 0/1/NA is available as gc_sex under debug/full_detail), and a submitted measurement of 0 is returned as 0 (not NaN). A value gc cannot use (uninterpretable sex; 0 or negative measurement) is flagged Exclude-Missing-Info but still returned as submitted. If tri_exclude = TRUE, a tri_exclude column is also included ("Include", "Same-Day", or "Exclude"). If cf_detail = TRUE, cf_status, cf_deltaz, cf_nextz, imperial, cf_dage_orig, and cf_dage_next columns are also included. cf_status labels every row ("Orig", "CF-R", "CF-NR", "CF-E", or "Not-CF"); adult rows are labeled "Adult" (no CF concept). For adult rows the three design-aid columns (imperial, cf_dage_orig, cf_dage_next) are NA. If corr_detail = TRUE, corr_val (per-value) and corr_pt (per-subject) correction-status columns are also included ("Corrected"/"Uncorrected"/"Adult"). If recenter_detail = TRUE, the recentered CSD z-score columns tbc.sd, ctbc.sd, and rcmedian.sd are also included (child rows only; NA for adult rows) as a stable output contract. If debug = TRUE, intermediate z-score diagnostic columns (sd.orig, sd.corr, tbc.sd, ctbc.sd) plus the perturbation z-scores (tbc.p_plus, tbc.p_minus, ctbc.p_plus, ctbc.p_minus) are retained for child rows (NA for adult rows), plus gc_sex (gc's interpreted integer sex, 0/1/NA, used by the algorithm; the verbatim submission stays in sex). If full_detail = TRUE, all of the above optional columns are included. Unless unit_error_range = FALSE, a unit_error_range logical column is also included (on by default; informational, never an exclusion).

Column naming: The six data columns (id, subjid, param, agedays, sex, measurement) are renamed to match the user's original argument expressions when those can be resolved to a simple name. For example, cleangrowth(subjid = dt\$patid, ...) returns a column named patid. Simple bare-name references (e.g., subjid = patient_vec) are also preserved. Complex expressions (e.g., function calls, literals) fall back to the canonical name.

The full catalog of exclusion codes (child and adult, covering both the Summary exclude values and the step-level exclude_detailed values) is documented in vignette("exclusion-codes").

Details

  1. Data-frame style (recommended). Pass a single data.frame or data.table as data. The subjid, param, agedays, sex, measurement, and id arguments are then column-name strings identifying which columns in data to use. Each defaults to the canonical name (e.g. "subjid"), so if your columns already use those names you can just call cleangrowth(data = my_df). Use the column-name arguments only to remap (e.g. subjid = "patid").

  2. Vector style (legacy). Omit data and pass each of subjid, param, agedays, sex, and measurement as a vector. Retained for backward compatibility with existing code; new code should use the data-frame style.

All vectors (or the columns referenced in data) must have the same length and be aligned row-for-row. Pulling values from different datasets is not supported and not checked for.

Examples

# \donttest{
# Data-frame style (recommended): canonical column names, just pass the
# data.frame. Add an `id` column for best results.
df_stats <- as.data.frame(syngrowth)
df_stats <- df_stats[df_stats$subjid %in% unique(df_stats[, "subjid"])[1:5], ]
df_stats$id <- seq_len(nrow(df_stats))
clean_stats <- cleangrowth(data = df_stats)

# Data-frame style with remapped column names
my_df <- df_stats
names(my_df)[names(my_df) == "subjid"] <- "patid"
clean_stats <- cleangrowth(data = my_df, subjid = "patid")

# Vector style (legacy)
clean_stats <- cleangrowth(subjid = df_stats$subjid,
                           param = df_stats$param,
                           agedays = df_stats$agedays,
                           sex = df_stats$sex,
                           measurement = df_stats$measurement,
                           id = df_stats$id)

# Parallel processing: run using 2 cores and batches
clean_stats <- cleangrowth(data = df_stats,
                           parallel = TRUE,
                           num.batches = 2)
# }

Contents

Developed by Carrie Daymont, Robert Grundmeier, Jeffrey Miller, Diego Campos, Dan Chudnov, Hannah De los Santos.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.1.
